programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a Navy commander last seen in the company of a mysterious blonde woman, who turns out to be missing NCIS agent and Tony's ex-lover EJ Barrett. programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A jet-skier crashes on to the shore and is discovered to be dead, having been shot through the chest. At HQ, Hetty shows the team surveillance footage of the shooting and identifies the victim as Daniel Su, an engineer who defected from North Korea and was working for the US government on a highly classified research project. Sam and Callen visit the victim's apartment, where they find the body of a woman on the floor.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine dies while saving the life of a foreign defence secretary, who was the target of an assassination attempt. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray and Michael Weatherly.(n)

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A woman is murdered at the home of Gunnery Sergeant Holt Perkins, and while the victim is initially assumed to be the marine's wife, analysis reveals her to be a convicted thief, presumed to be on the scene as part of a plot to hit Perkins' house. When the agents discover the calling card of notorious terror cell Dragon's Tooth on the body, it becomes clear the murder has been committed as part of a wider agenda, and the team joins forces with the Department of Defence to catch the killer. American drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo and Pauley Perrette.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine Brandon Valdivia falls to his death from a rooftop bar and is initially thought to have committed suicide, but his family refuse to accept that possibility and persuade a congressman to put some pressure on the admiral. Sam and Callen are then brought in to re-evaluate the case and discover that Valdivia may have been involved in money laundering with a fellow marine.(n)

programme.title: NCIS

programme.desc: A prostitute and what seems to be one of her clients are gunned down in a drive-by shooting, and when it emerges that the would-be John was actually a Navy officer, Gibbs and DiNozzo realise the killings are connected Lex Tarlionas, an incomplete operation to bring justice to those responsible for an attack on a US airport in 1979, which left 100 people dead. The main suspect is a former Colombian drugs kingpin known as Tomas Orlando, and the murdered sex worker was about to hand over crucial DNA evidence that could have connected him to the crime, but now that she is dead, Gibbs and the others must find another way to finally bring Orlando in. American drama, starring Mark Harmon and David McCallum.(n)

programme.desc: A US Navy lieutenant is murdered at the Defence Logistics Agency, while visitings the office of an admiral currently away at sea. As the quest to locate the missing artwork gets under way, the agents realise they need the help of an expert. programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah returns to Inverness with Rene, but may have underestimated her feelings for George. Olivia finds the burden of deception weighs heavily, while Anna and Gino prepare for their wedding day.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: To Catch a Killer - Part Three
programme.desc: Part three of three. A DNA sample seems to rule out cabbie David Russo as the murderer of nurse Manisha Devi - until it transpires that he has cancer and recently underwent a blood transfusion that could have affected the test result. Peter Baxter is stabbed by a traumatised Ruth Cohen, while June has a run-in with a 13-year-old arson suspect and later calls the station to report a rape - naming herself as victim.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Abra supervises Diane as he covers for Ric for a fortnight, and reveals he would be keen to work at the hospital permanently - but his determination to perform an operation tempts him to bend the rules, and ruins his chances. Tricia tells Chrissie about her relationship with Mark, and decides to have her surgery moved forward.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Josh and Penny are called to deliver a baby in unusual circumstances, and Max faces his worst nightmare as his son is brought in for emergency treatment. Meanwhile, identical twins are reunited for the first time in 28 years by an accident. Medical drama, starring Ian Bleasdale and Donna Alexander.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Teenage rapist Ross Trescot is brought into custody, but his arrest does little to comfort June, who insists in being interviewed by Sheelagh, unable to bear the thought of reliving her ordeal with a stranger. Yvonne also faces a stressful time as she gives evidence at the inquest into Lee Thomas's death. Colleagues support her during the day, but a verdict of unlawful killing is recorded and she faces the unsettling prospect of being charged with murder. Away from the line of duty, Phil Hunter grows increasingly uneasy as Kate plans their wedding and finally admits he is still married to Cindy - prompting her to throw him out of the house.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Arthur frets over not being able to see his son after the fall out of Pauline finding out about his affair. Michelle confront Ms. Hewitt about wreaking her parents marriage.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Arthur and Chrissy are both having marriage problems. Will Adrian and Mandy get jobs to put food on the table. Sanjay know Geetas sister Meena is up to no good!(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Blue Watch return to the scene of a fire in the basement of a house converted into bedsits. Meanwhile, Recall says goodbye to his family as they move to Scotland without him.(n)

programme.title: Monarch of the Glen
programme.desc: Paul's errant uncle Donald MacDonald returns to Glenbogle after a 40-year absence, but his homecoming receives a mixed reaction. Lexie sets out to forget her marital problems by organising Molly's 60th birthday party, but the matriarch is not so keen to celebrate. Meanwhile, Isobel faces financial ruin after a contract to supply the estate with fresh bread and vegetables is cancelled, and Jess is concerned that she and Duncan are turning into a boring couple. Drama, guest starring Tom Baker, with Lloyd Owen, Susan Hampshire and Dawn Steele.(n)

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Karen is subjected to a horrific assault by the grandson of a pensioner in her care, while Luke gets into trouble at school and responds by running away, plunging Anna into a panic. On a happier note, Tom clinches an important business deal with German client Herr Gruber, while Joe contemplates moving in with Beth. Leslie Ash, Julian Lewis Jones and Lesley Dunlop star.(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey makes an appointment with a Harley Street specialist, hoping to allay her suspicions that she may be pregnant. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph.(n)

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Marlene discovers the farmhands concocting a virility potion for Rocky the bull, and manages to sneak a dose into Boycie's coffee. Make-up is found in Tyler's room, and his parents are relieved to be told he has a girlfriend - until it transpires she is also his teacher. John Challis, Sue Holderness and Jack Doolan star.(n)

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Jean is looking forward to the pitter-patter of tiny feet - despite the fact that daughter Judith is not even pregnant.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo tracks down his missing ferret to Nora's washing basket, but smuggling it out unnoticed proves tricky. Bill Owen and Kathy Staff star.(n)

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Lynley and Havers investigate when young lawyer Emily Proctor is found axed to death in Hyde Park. They are led to the chambers of a human rights barrister who was having an affair with her, and it isn't long before the victim's seedy double life involving Internet porn and dark family secrets emerges. Detective drama, starring Nathaniel Parker, Sharon Small and Samuel West.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Jack announces he is quitting the team, but before Sandra, Brian and Gerry can question his reasons, shadowy Whitehall figure Stephen Fisher arrives with one of his secret cases - the unsolved murder of a woman dating back more than 160 years. With just 24 hours to solve it, will the sleuths find time to get to the bottom of Jack's shock decision and persuade him to stick around?(n)

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The team discovers a DNA match between a medical student reported missing six years previously and a corpse that was found at around the same time. Investigations into the man's background reveal he had been suspected of having links to terrorist organisations - but Boyd believes a high-level cover-up is obscuring the whole truth about the case. Guest starring Don Warrington.(n)

programme.title: The White Princess
programme.desc: Lizzie must deal with her mother's bold defence of the York cause, while the appearance of a boy in Burgundy claiming to be a young prince threatens to further destabilise King Henry's rule. King Henry's mother Lady Margaret Beaufort faces the consequences of harbouring a dark secret. Jodie Comer, Jacob Collins-Levy, Suki Waterhouse and Michelle Fairley star.(n)

programme.title: The White Princess
programme.desc: King Henry VII and Lizzie travel to Spain to secure their son's betrothal, but find news of their country's instability has preceded them. Meanwhile, unrest growing in Burgundy spills over into an English battle. Jodie Comer, Jacob Collins-Levy, Suki Waterhouse and Michelle Fairley star.(n)

Meanwhile, jealousy gets the better of Kyla, who berates Justin for behaving unprofessionally after catching him flirting with Donna.(n)

programme.desc: Max gets in the way of the struggle to save his son Frank's life. Meanwhile, a bizarre coincidence reunites a mother and son, and Charlie's least favourite patient causes a scene. Hospital drama, starring Derek Thompson, Robert Gwilym and Ian Bleasdale.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: At the End of a Long Day
programme.desc: Yvonne sets off for Barton Street Station to face formal manslaughter charges but has a run-in with a drunk en route and ends up hitting him - which won't exactly count in her favour when she appears in court. Jo Masters discovers one of Phil's informants is dealing in drugs with the collusion of a crooked customs officer, while Roger and Dan find a missing schoolgirl in woodlands and realise she has just given birth - but can't see any sign of her baby.(n)

programme.desc: Hattie is trying to find a solution about working for Ian. Pauline refuses to have Arthur back. Gita would like to visit her sick mother in India.(n)

programme.desc: Chrissy has a plan but wants Arthur to support her, Frank is going out of his mind trying to find out news about his pregnant daughter.(n)

programme.desc: Bayleaf hands in his resignation. Meanwhile, the fire-fighters are called to a fire at a registry office, and Remy and Kevin quarrel about Mickey.(n)

programme.title: Monarch of the Glen
programme.desc: The legacy of Paul and Lexie's secret kiss strains relations between them, but despite initial reservations, she reconsiders her decision to leave Glenbogle. Duncan decides the time is right for him to make changes in his life, and steps in to help a begrudging Ewan out when an accident incapacitates him. Meanwhile, Duncan and Molly reveal details of a hidden past that threaten to have dramatic implications for the rest of the family.(n)

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Anna receives a poignant reminder of her painful break-up with Simon and the Lampards agonise over whether to let Oscar's real dad see him, afraid of the emotional consequences. Joe takes Beth's rejection badly, while Karen has a traumatic encounter with Garth at Tessie Banks' funeral and later finds herself forced to save his life in a tense scene on the edge of windswept Blackscar Top.(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Dorien may have ulterior motives for inviting Tracey to a posh event, while Sharon makes a tidy profit out of fake Rolling Stones T-shirts. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph.(n)

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie secretly buys a load of artificial fertiliser to help his supposedly organic crops - but is seen unloading it by a neighbour, who promptly phones the Agricultural Office to complain about his behaviour. Starring John Challis.(n)

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Jean and Lionel are eager for Sandy and Harry's return from Canada.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Sid attempts to install a doorbell in his cafe, so Compo, Clegg and Foggy try to assist - with disastrous consequences. John Comer, Peter Sallis, Brian Wilde and Bill Owen star in Roy Clarke's long-running comedy.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The circus brings death to Kembleford when a clown is murdered, and Father Brown hunts the killer while trying to determine why a parishioner has agreed to marry the sinister wizard Welkin. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams as a Roman Catholic priest who attempts to solve various misdemeanours.(n)

programme.title: The Doctor Blake Mysteries
programme.desc: Doctor Blake has to piece together arson and a fatal shooting of a farmer. Meanwhile, a leak to the paper concerning the medic creates difficulties for his relationship with Jean. Australian drama, starring Craig McLachlan.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: In their first case without trusty colleague Jack, the team members reinvestigate the disappearance of PE teacher Jason Bowe when the remains of a body are discovered near the elite public boarding school where he taught. Unfortunately, their snooping could not have come at a worse time for the staff, who are preparing to welcome a local MP to open their new computer centre - but what they do glean tells them the dead man seemed very much a loner. However, it soon turns out he got on much better with his students, forming close and inappropriate relationships with several. But could his behaviour have rattled someone enough to kill him? Susannah Harker and Nicola Walker guest star.(n)

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Boyd learns he is being removed from the Cold Case unit. For his final job, he re-opens an investigation into the disappearance of 16 homeless teenagers between 1979 and 1982 - the first case he ever worked on. However, as the team struggles to find any new information, Grace worries about her colleague's reasons for revisiting his past - while a guilt-ridden Sarah becomes desperate to make amends for her role in Boyd's sacking. Sarring Trevor Eve, with Paul McGann.(n)

programme.title: The White Princess
programme.desc: A pretender to the throne is held captive at court, confounding the Tudors, and creating a new rift in Lizzie and King Henry's marriage. Lizzie faces an impossible choice, and makes a risky move to try to eliminate the threat to her family. Jodie Comer, Jacob Collins-Levy, Suki Waterhouse and Michelle Fairley star.(n)

programme.title: The White Princess
programme.desc: The pretender exposes King Henry's weaknesses, while Lizzie confronts new demons and old curses. Concluding the sequel to The White Queen, set in the Middle Ages as the War of the Roses comes to an end. Jodie Comer, Jacob Collins-Levy, Suki Waterhouse and Michelle Fairley star.(n)

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: Janet's maternal instincts are aroused by the discovery of a day-old baby abandoned on the doorstep of Arden House. Medical drama, with Annette Crosbie and David Rintoul.(n)

programme.desc: A flag from the American War of Independence turns up in a local church and triggers a wave of public interest as a number of villagers come forward to claim it as their own. Light-hearted drama, starring Ian McShane and Phyllis Logan.(n)

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The roguish dealer tries to beat Charlie Gimbert to a priceless collection of Jewish antiques that has turned up in Essex. Alfred Marks guest stars with Ian McShane.(n)

programme.desc: Sean Bean returns as Bernard Cornwell's swashbuckling hero, this time setting out to rescue British deserters captured by Spanish rebel El Casco. Along the way he crosses swords with a fellow officer and come to the aid of a damsel in distress. Daragh O'Malley, Hugh Fraser and John Tams also star.(n)

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Oz is forced to take radical action to ensure the boys do not come out of the bridge job penniless, but it seems unlikely that his unique brand of sexual charm will save the day - and his doubts about son Rod's masculinity do not do much to help his focus. Just as they seem doomed to fail, a mysterious stranger arrives. Dick Clement and Ian La Frenais's comedy drama, with Tim Healy, Jimmy Nail and Timothy Spall.(n)

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Joe Saugus throws the lads a monetary lifeline, offering to buy the transporter bridge on behalf of his people, while Barry has a final showdown with Kadi and Tatiana. Believing their financial worries are finally over, the lads set off to oversee the construction process in Arizona - where a hostile reception awaits them. Timothy Spall, Jimmy Nail, Tim Healy and Bill Nighy star.(n)

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Laura and Rosemary are hired to create a restaurant garden on Italy's Ligurian coast, but it is not long before their working holiday is disrupted by a murder. Determined to solve the crime before returning home, they find themselves with no shortage of suspects - and matters are complicated further by a second death. Guest starring Suzanne Bertish (Rome) and Tristan Gemmill (Casualty), with Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris.(n)

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: An archaeologist posing as Rosemary dies mysteriously during a dig for Anglo-Saxon artefacts in the grounds of Lord Engleton's stately home, presenting the duo with a fresh mystery to solve. The gardens are unaccountably blighted by yellowing, forcing the estate to close its gates to the public, and a second death suggests somebody will go to any lengths for a piece of British history. Guest starring Julian Wadham.(n)

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Laura and Rosemary are called in to investigate when gardens entered for a village contest are attacked by a disease. Further sinister undercurrents are exposed when reigning champion Donald Westward is found dead and recriminations start to fly as buried rivalries come to the surface. Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris star.(n)

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: A series of murders occurs after Murdoch and Ogden's book is released, prompting a desperate search for the copycat killer.(n)

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: After a 10-year-old girl is injured in a petrol bomb attack by animal rights activists, an outraged Gene takes Alex to interview a veteran campaigner in jail. Though the prisoner is weak and incoherent after going on hunger strike, Alex is convinced he not only has details of planned attacks - he may also know about her own situation and be able to help her get home. Guest starring David Bradley and Rory Kinnear.(n)

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: Gene receives a visit from Jackie Queen, a journalist from Manchester, who announces to the shocked superintendent she is pregnant with his baby. She is also investigating the disappearance of teenagers suspected of running away to London, so the team launches an investigation and a suspect is quickly identified. However, it becomes clear the man is being protected by someone within the Met. John Bowe and Ruth Millar guest star.(n)

programme.title: Kavanagh QC

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers test out three new products, including a vegan skincare range, a coat for drying dogs, and a device designed to attach items of hand luggage together.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return to Compton Verney, Warwickshire. Finds include hand-painted fashion illustrations from the 1930s, a pair of bronze cockerels from Benin, West Africa, and a rare Chinese incense burner worth a small fortune. Fiona meets the granddaughter of the first British woman to win an individual swimming gold medal, at the 1924 Olympics, and military expert Mark Smith hears the story of how a First World War hero earned the Victoria Cross.(n)

programme.title: Kate Humble - Into the Volcano
programme.desc: Part one of two. Kate Humble joins a team of geologists on a research expedition to the Vanuatu archipelago in the southern Pacific, beginning at Mount Yasur on the island of Tanna, one of the most active volcanos in the world. To discover whether a devastating eruption might happen, the scientists need to collect a lava bomb the minute it is hurled from the crater and while they prepare for their mission, Kate explores the island and discovers how the volcano shapes the lives, traditions and beliefs of everyone who lives in its shadow.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Alex Polizzi: Chefs on Trial
programme.desc: The mission to find an executive head chef for Lake District restaurant The Gilpin continues. The last three candidates prepare a partridge fit for a fine-dining menu - and with only one place left in tomorrow's final, the pressure is on. While one of the chefs sets out to create something deserving of a Michelin star, another has trouble convincing the family he really wants the position, when it turns out he never stays in one job very long.(n)

programme.title: The War Lords
programme.desc: British historian AJP Taylor appraises the performance of the Second World War leaders, with this edition focusing on the role of Hitler during the conflict.(n)

programme.title: World Snooker: Crucible Classics
programme.desc: John Higgins v Judd Trump. Hazel Irvine introduces action from the 2011 World Championship final, staged at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team is in Old College, Aberystwyth University, on the west coast of Wales, where Christina Trevanion values a pair of Wedgwood plates, while a gold guinea impresses Raj Bisram.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Comedians Al Murray and Nish Kumar, presenter Anneka Rice and Radio 1 DJ Clara Amfo continue the daily test of their general knowledge skills, hosted by Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: International sheepdog breeder David and farmer Brenda battle the temperamental British weather with their pedigree dogs by their sides, competing in Powburn, Northumberland, at one of Britain's oldest sheepdog trials.(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: Mary Beard and her guests consider the effects of Covid-19 on the world of literature, asking whether isolation will lead to a post-pandemic rise in books. There is also a discussion on how writing and drama chronicled the HIV and Aids crisis of the 1980s and helped to shape public attitudes.(n)

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: Coniston Water isn't the biggest of Cumbria's lakes - but it's one of the most iconic and the place where Donald Campbell died trying to break the world water speed record. Paul meets Campbell's daughter Gina and crew member Robbie Robinson, who was there on the fateful day in January 1967 when Bluebird somersaulted and crashed on the water. The explorer also enters a Lakeland bike ride with a difference before ending his visit with a spot of rock climbing at Dow Crag before the ascent of one of the area's best known hills, The Old Man of Coniston.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Susan Calman presents as the Welsh chefs serve up their mains and desserts inspired by children's literature. The dishes include a Boggis, Bunce and Bean Farmers' Supper based on Fantastic Mr Fox, a guinea fowl inspired by the legend of Welsh dragon Y Ddraig Goch, a Willy Wonka golden egg and a simple marmalade sandwich in homage to everyone's favourite bear from darkest Peru, Paddington.(n)

programme.title: Devs
programme.desc: Stewart feels uptight when he is begged for a favour, especially as he has grave misgivings about Forest and the project. Lily is schooled in the ideas behind Devs, and later - when she emerges from heavy sedation - she realises it is not feasible to flee her problems. Instead she elects to make a house call, but does not get to question the person she expected.(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Darren Harriott hosts the show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo, warming up the audience and then introducing stand-up performances by Jessica Fostekew and Stephen Bailey.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Big Animal Surgery
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin enters the world of wild animal surgery, beginning at a sanctuary in South Africa, where she witnesses an extraordinary operation inside the jaws of a lion. Nine-year-old Ricci has problems with his teeth so severe that he is unable to feed properly. One of the world's leading vets is on hand to fix the problem, using an array of specially made tools. But things take a dangerous twist when the patient begins to wake up from the anaesthetic midway through.(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Five talented amateurs compete to win a place in the finals. Each must cook an outstanding dish to win over four of the country's most feared restaurant critics - Grace Dent, William Sitwell, Jay Rayner and Jimi Famurewa. Serving food to these masters of the dining room is a daunting task for the most adept chef, and these intrepid home cooks now have to put their skills and palates on the line. Any mistake could cost them the competition, so with the pressure firmly on it promises to be a tense and thrilling affair. At the end, judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace will no doubt face a difficult decision.(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Mediterranean Adventure
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the culinary delights of Provence in southern France. Starting out in Marseille, they meet a Moroccan woman who runs a welcome centre where immigrants can eat together - and who makes a fish stew to die for. Travelling west, they learn about the culinary influence of the Romans who conquered Europe 2,000 years ago, try a breakfast of pastry filled with spicy octopus, and dine with a Catalan farmer and his family on a regional speciality of barbecued snails.(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers test and score an eco-lunchbox, an all-in-one waterproof coat for children and a cafetiere accessory that avoids having to scoop up the mushy grains when finished.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a foundry in France that produces iron pots and pans at the rate of one every five seconds, while Cherry Healey is in South Africa, at one of the world's largest iron ore mines, and rides in a giant dumper truck. Historian Ruth Goodman explains how cooking in pots has evolved, from communal ovens to 1970s slow cookers.(n)

programme.title: Kate Humble - Into the Volcano
programme.desc: Part two of two. Kate Humble joins a team of geologists on another research expedition in the southern Pacific as they head to the island of Ambrym in the Vanuatu archipelago. There she undertakes an arduous journey to the volcanic cone Marum - which contains one of only five lava lakes on the planet - abseiling right to its heart to discover if another major eruption may be imminent. Along the way she discovers how the volcano has shaped the customs and traditions of the islanders and discovers what it really means to live each day on its slopes.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Road to Mexico
programme.desc: The chef continues down the Californian coastline immortalised by John Steinbeck, a region of citrus groves and vineyards. On the edge of Los Angeles, Rick discovers Olvera Street, where the Hispanic origins of the city took root. Then heading south to San Diego, he meets 79-year-old Peter Halmay, the oldest sea-urchin diver in town, and is served the best fish chilli he has ever had by a local fishmonger.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The latest two highest-scoring chefs representing Wales cook their six-course menus again to impress judges Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The arbitrators are joined by actor David Bradley - Filch from the Harry Potter films - to help them determine who to send through to the national finals. Presented by Susan Calman.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: The green-fingered team guides team enthusiasts through seasonal highlights from across the country visiting stunning gardens, meeting the gardeners and finding out their secrets of success. Monty Don and faithful hounds Nigel and Nellie are at Longmeadow to bring inspiration as well as outline all the essential gardening jobs for this weekend. Cameras also travel the UK, visiting stunning gardens, meeting the gardeners and finding out their secrets of success.(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Bill Bailey, Katherine Ryan and Rhod Gilbert join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig for an unconventional look at the theme 'Operations'. During the course of the show, the panellists find out how to turn a muffin into an offensive weapon. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window, as the fiendishly difficult questions lead to points being awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct.(n)

programme.title: The Mash Report
programme.desc: A self-isolated Nish Kumar hosts Britain's most uncompromising topical satire, tackling all the troubling news from a world that's put its faith in Boris Johnson and Donald Trump. His similarly quarantined correspondents include Rachel Parris, Geoff Norcott and Ellie Taylor.(n)

programme.title: The Mini Mash Report
programme.desc: Highlights from the satirical news show, presented by Nish Kumar, as a cast of comedy correspondents give their take on the week's events.(n)

programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories, with Kirsty Wark.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Oranges and Sunshine (2010)
programme.desc: A social worker discovers that, over several decades, thousands of British children have been falsely informed their parents are dead and been forcibly sent to care homes in Australia, where they suffered terrible abuse. She devotes herself to exposing the scandal and helping the victims to find their families. Fact-based drama, with Emily Watson, Hugo Weaving and David Wenham.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Has The Government Failed The NHS? - Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines.(n)

programme.title: Cops Like Us
programme.desc: The Staffordshire Police officers look to the future as they take on new recruits, including trainee Holly Swift. After working in mental health services and seeing the difference police officers can make, she decided it was the career for her. At the other end of the career path is Sgt Dave Allan - who is just about to complete 30 years' service and retire. The police service recruited heavily after the miners' strikes in the 1980s, and many of those officers are now approaching retirement, including Dave.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood show clips from the natural world, including an elephant seal that turned up on a zebra crossing in Rio de Janeiro and a moose stuck up a tree.(n)

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: The most unusual places in the UK.(n)

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Joe Tracini and Meryl Fernandes enlist expert designers and builders to construct dream dens for lucky youngsters.(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme.(n)

programme.title: Monsters vs Aliens: Mutant Pumpkins from Outer Space
programme.desc: Halloween-themed spin-off from DreamWorks' blockbuster animated movie. Aliens disguise themselves as pumpkins as part of a fiendish plot to take over the world - but Susan Murphy and her team of monsters are ready to fight back. With the voices of Reese Witherspoon, Seth Rogen and Hugh Laurie.(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall and the crew head to Sri Lanka in search of dangerous predators, hunting for blue whales and tracking down Asian elephants. Along the way they encounter two deadly snakes, a venomous Russell's viper and the enormous Indian rock python.(n)

programme.title: Astronauts: Do You Have What It Takes?
programme.desc: The remaining candidates head to a secret facility in Sweden. There they are deliberately deprived of oxygen to see if they can recognise the signs of hypoxia, critical for an astronaut on a spacewalk. Their ability to withstand the strong G-forces of space travel is also tested in a human centrifuge.(n)

programme.title: Operation Wild
programme.desc: Clare Balding travels to Japan where she visits inventor and vet Dr Keiichi Ueda. The vet has dedicated the past 12 years of his career to helping a dolphin called Fuji who has no tail as a result of a bacterial infection and he is hoping that a new prosthetic tail will enable her to swim properly again. Steve Leonard heads to the rainforest of Laos, where Edinburgh Zoo vet Romain Pizzi is undertaking keyhole surgery on an endangered moon bear called Champa who suffers from excess fluid on her brain. Steve then visits Florida where reptile specialist Doug Mader is using the latest technology to save Martha, an alligator with a badly blocked gut. Clare and Steve also follow the stories of a Galapagos tortoise with a mystery illness, a five-ton elephant suffering from an infected tusk and a tiger that keeps having nose bleeds.(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The wildlife of the Highlands, seen through the eyes of naturalist Fergus Beeley, who spent a year at Loch Maree and the surrounding hills. He follows the fortunes of rare black-throated divers and white-tailed sea eagles, and films the red deer and salmon whose lives revolve around the loch.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Susan Calman presents as the Welsh chefs serve up their starters and fish courses inspired by children's literature, including an homage to the legend of Twrch Trwyth and an ode to Roald Dahl. The chefs' fate depends on the scores they receive from their veteran and, with one person leaving after the fish course, nerves are running high. With some surprising scores all round, who will be eliminated from the competition and not cook their entire menu? The pressure is on for all the chefs to deliver.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Susan Calman presents as the Welsh chefs serve up their mains and desserts inspired by children's literature. The dishes include a Boggis, Bunce and Bean Farmers' Supper based on Fantastic Mr Fox, a guinea fowl inspired by the legend of Welsh dragon Y Ddraig Goch, a Willy Wonka golden egg and a simple marmalade sandwich in homage to everyone's favourite bear from darkest Peru, Paddington.(n)

programme.title: World Snooker: Crucible Classics
programme.desc: Neil Robertson v Judd Trump. Hazel Irvine introduces action from the quarter-final at the 2014 World Championship, staged at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield.(n)

programme.title: Talking Snooker
programme.desc: Alistair McGowan looks at the players who helped increase the sport's popularity, including Steve Davis, Stephen Hendry and Dennis Taylor.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The contestants are asked to insert an invisible zip and pockets on a pair of palazzo pants, while the judges keep a keen eye on how well they pattern-match the bold prints. For the transformation challenge, the sewers must turn towels into stylish beach cover-ups in just 90 minutes. Male models arrive in the Sewing Room for the final challenge, ready to be fitted with a made-to-measure holiday shirt using bright and colourful fabrics. Whose shirt will bring on the sunshine, and who will be sent packing?(n)

programme.title: We're Doomed! The Dad's Army Story
programme.desc: Comedy drama based on the story of how Jimmy Perry and David Croft brought the sitcom Dad's Army to life. Running from Perry's initial idea in 1967 until the transmission of the first episode in 1968, the film shows the beginnings of their writing partnership and the casting woes, personal clashes and production difficulties that put the show's existence in jeopardy. Starring Paul Ritter and Richard Dormer.(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Mainwaring's Home Guard heroes try to raise money for the local Spitfire fund by having a bash at morris dancing for a carnival. Meanwhile, Jones is worried that Mrs Fox is falling for the town clerk, so he asks the captain if he can help. Classic comedy first broadcast in 1974, starring Arthur Lowe, John Le Mesurier and Clive Dunn.(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo tours the imperial city of Hue, crosses the former demilitarised zone that once separated North from South Vietnam, and takes a boat ride on the Red River Delta. Approaching Vietnamese capital Hanoi aboard the Unification Railway, Michael passes the famous street where the locomotive passes within centimetres of the houses either side of the tracks. He also visits a former Soviet factory that once supplied military uniforms to the North Vietnamese army and Viet Cong, which now makes sportswear for the United States Olympic team. He ends this leg at the limestone islets and turquoise waters of Halong Bay.(n)

programme.title: The Guernsey Literary and Potato Peel Pie Society (2018)
programme.desc: In London, 1946, author Juliet Ashton's career has taken off but she is not enjoying promoting her detective stories to eager audiences on a book tour. When she receives a letter from a Guernsey pig farmer inviting her to attend a meeting of his literary society, she eagerly accepts. What Juliet discovers about her new friends and their secrets leads to her staying on the island much longer than she planned. Drama, starring Lily James, Tom Courtenay and Katherine Parkinson.(n)

programme.title: Lee Miller - A Life on the Front Line
programme.desc: Lee Miller is one of the most remarkable female icons of the 20th century. A fashion model-turned-photographer, then later a war reporter, Miller chose to live life by her own rules. This documentary drama celebrates a subject who defied anyone who tried to pin her down, put her on a pedestal or pigeonhole her in any way. Often at odds with the morality of the day, she refused to be subjugated by the dominant male figures around her.(n)

programme.title: Spitfire Women
programme.desc: The story of the 168 female pilots who served in Britain's Air Transport Auxiliary - an organisation responsible for delivering aircraft to the RAF on the front lines of battle during the Second World War. Despite having no radio or navigation equipment, ATA pilots were expected to fly any kind of aircraft to the destinations where they were most needed, often risking their lives in the process. The film also features interviews with the last surviving female veterans, who speak about the dangers they encountered in the skies and the discrimination they faced back home.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints another signature landscape, featuring a mountain, cabin and meadow, as seen through a window.(n)

programme.title: Museums in Quarantine
programme.desc: Art historian Janina Ramirez takes a virtual tour of the British Museum, whose galleries she has paced since she was a girl. Here she highlights objects as varied as an Aztec decorated skull, ancient Egyptian cat mummies and an 18th-century tea set.(n)

programme.title: Great Expectations (2012)
programme.desc: A penniless orphan is chosen by a reclusive spinster to be a playmate for her cold-hearted ward, whom he falls in love with. As he grows up, a mysterious benefactor offers him the chance to rise into high society, but dark secrets lie behind both the object of his adoration and his newfound fortune. Period drama based on Charles Dickens' novel, starring Jeremy Irvine, Helena Bonham Carter, Ralph Fiennes and Holliday Grainger.(n)

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic examines techniques and conventions behind science fiction films, including 2001: A Space Odyssey, Blade Runner, Arrival, Gravity and one of his favourites, Silent Running. He argues that for all their spectacle, sci-fi films ultimately derive their power from being about us.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Scientists and doctors investigate the properties of human hair and examine the methods and results of research done for business that are developing hair care products. They reveal whether or not it is worth splashing out on expensive shampoos and also uncover the special ingredients found in conditioners, and lay bare the secrets of the shiny, glossy hair seen in adverts.(n)

programme.title: Novels That Shaped Our World
programme.desc: The second documentary examining fictional writing from three different perspectives considers the subject of race and colonialism. It looks at books including Robinson Crusoe, the first novel ever published in English, in 1719, whose hero was a slave trader, and two famous American novels published more than a century apart - Harriet Beecher Stowe's Uncle Tom's Cabin and Toni Morrison's Beloved. The programme also comes up to date with titles such as Malorie Blackman's Noughts and Crosses and Paul Beatty's The Sell Out.(n)

programme.title: Arena
programme.sub-title: Arena: Nicolas Roeg - It's About Time
programme.desc: Profile of the acclaimed director of Don't Look Now, The Man who Fell to Earth, Walkabout and Performance. Roeg reflects on the recurring themes of his work, including perception of time and the difficulty of human relationships. With contributions from actors Donald Sutherland, Julie Christie, Jenny Agutter and Theresa Russell, and film-makers who have been inspired by his work, including Danny Boyle and Ben Wheatley.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints another signature landscape, featuring a mountain, cabin and meadow, as seen through a window.(n)

programme.title: Museums in Quarantine
programme.desc: Art historian Janina Ramirez takes a virtual tour of the British Museum, whose galleries she has paced since she was a girl. Here she highlights objects as varied as an Aztec decorated skull, ancient Egyptian cat mummies and an 18th-century tea set.(n)

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic examines techniques and conventions behind science fiction films, including 2001: A Space Odyssey, Blade Runner, Arrival, Gravity and one of his favourites, Silent Running. He argues that for all their spectacle, sci-fi films ultimately derive their power from being about us.(n)

programme.title: Virtuoso Violinists at the BBC
programme.desc: From the Royal Academy of Music Museum in London, Nicola Benedetti explores 60 years of BBC archive to celebrate the world of the violin and its most outstanding performers. From Nathan Milstein, Mischa Elman and Isaac Stern to Yehudi Menuhin, Itzhak Perlman and Nigel Kennedy, the presenter offers a violinist's perspective on what makes a great performance in a tradition that stretches back to the 19th-century virtuoso Paganini.(n)

programme.title: Tunes for Tyrants: Music & Power with Suzy Klein
programme.desc: Suzy reaches the 1930s, when the totalitarian dictators sought to use and abuse music for ideological ends. She looks at the lives of Richard Strauss, Dmitri Shostakovich and Sergei Prokofiev, who produced some of the 20th-century's best-loved music whilst navigating the precarious tightrope of working for perhaps the most terrifying music lovers ever - Hitler and Stalin. The political message of the classic musical fairy tale Peter and the Wolf is revealed as well as the secret code hidden in Shostakovich's quartets and Strauss's deeply personal reasons for trying to please the Nazis.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Steve Wright and Jenny Powell host the edition first shown on August 3, featuring performances by Martika, Paul McCartney, Transvision Vamp, Lightning Seeds, Kylie Minogue, Shakespears Sister and Jive Bunny and the Mastermixers.(n)

programme.title: The Shadows at Sixty
programme.desc: From where they began as the Drifters to the backing band for Cliff Richard, and on to their huge success as the Shadows, this documentary celebrates the band's achievements across 60 years in what has been a time of constant change within social, cultural and musical landscapes. The Shadows were at the forefront of the UK beat boom generation and the first backing band to emerge as big stars in their own right and this programme features unseen archive, personal testimony, interviews with Cliff Richard, band members Hank Marvin, Bruce Welch and Brian Bennett and some of those they influenced, including Brian May, David Gilmour and Pete Townshend. Narrated by Gina McKee.(n)

programme.title: Great Guitar Riffs at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of archive clips and performances which include some of the most memorable guitar riffs of all time. Featuring Jimi Hendrix, the Kinks, Cream, AC/DC, the Smiths, Rage Against the Machine, Radiohead, Foo Fighters, Pixies and the Stone Roses.(n)

programme.title: Elton John Live at Hyde Park
programme.desc: The singer performs a concert in London's Hyde Park to a crowd of 50,000 fans, as the headlining act of Radio 2's Festival in a Day event. The show features old favourites from Elton's five-decade career, as well as songs from his recent studio album Wonderful Crazy Night, on which he and Bernie Taupin, the songwriter behind many of his greatest hits, worked together for the first time in nearly 10 years.(n)

programme.title: People's History of Pop
programme.desc: Twiggy celebrates the decade from 1955, when the pop music fan was born, hearing from fans of the era. The stories uncovered include a young Lonnie Donegan admirer inviting the singer back to a friend's house where they enjoyed an impromptu skiffle jam, and a schoolgirl's whose dream came true when the Beatles turned up at the pub where her mother was a waitress. Plus, the experiences of those lucky to attend a recording of legendary music show Ready, Steady, Go!(n)

programme.title: The Shadows at Sixty
programme.desc: From where they began as the Drifters to the backing band for Cliff Richard, and on to their huge success as the Shadows, this documentary celebrates the band's achievements across 60 years in what has been a time of constant change within social, cultural and musical landscapes. The Shadows were at the forefront of the UK beat boom generation and the first backing band to emerge as big stars in their own right and this programme features unseen archive, personal testimony, interviews with Cliff Richard, band members Hank Marvin, Bruce Welch and Brian Bennett and some of those they influenced, including Brian May, David Gilmour and Pete Townshend. Narrated by Gina McKee.(n)

programme.title: Tunes for Tyrants: Music & Power with Suzy Klein
programme.desc: Suzy reaches the 1930s, when the totalitarian dictators sought to use and abuse music for ideological ends. She looks at the lives of Richard Strauss, Dmitri Shostakovich and Sergei Prokofiev, who produced some of the 20th-century's best-loved music whilst navigating the precarious tightrope of working for perhaps the most terrifying music lovers ever - Hitler and Stalin. The political message of the classic musical fairy tale Peter and the Wolf is revealed as well as the secret code hidden in Shostakovich's quartets and Strauss's deeply personal reasons for trying to please the Nazis.(n)

programme.title: Primates
programme.desc: Chris Packham narrates a programme looking at the family of mammals, beginning by revealing the extraordinary strategies monkeys, apes and lemurs must use to survive in the most unexpected places. In South Africa, rarely seen bush babies go in search of food in the lean times of winter, while times are also hard for a troop of bearded capuchins in Brazil's scorched badlands.(n)

programme.title: How the Wild West Was Won with Ray Mears
programme.desc: The adventurer explores how the American wilderness shaped the Wild West. He begins by looking at the landscapes of three great mountain ranges - the Appalachians, the Rockies and the Sierra Nevada - and how they challenged the westward push of early pioneers. He discovers how their geography, extreme weather, wild animals and ecology provided both opportunities and challenges for those living there, and also experiences mule-trekking and gold prospecting.(n)

programme.title: State of Happiness
programme.desc: It is December 1969 and international oil companies are pulling out of Stavanger in Norway after years of test drilling without success. Christian Nyman works as a diver on one of the rigs, while Toril Torstensen's American boyfriend works for Shell, but what happens next affects everyone in town. Starring Anne Regine Ellingsaeter, Amund Harboe, Toril Torstensen and Bart Edwards. In Norwegian and English.(n)

programme.title: State of Happiness
programme.desc: Phillips Petroleum introduces a smoking ban on the Ocean Viking rig, while the mayor of Stavanger speculates on whether the Americans might have found oil. Toril Torstensen makes an unavoidable choice. In Norwegian and English.(n)

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Music compilation show celebrating some of the most popular boy bands to have graced TV screens since the 1960s. programme.desc: Noddy Holder introduces a compilation of the band's performances from the BBC archives. programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Tommy Boyd, Andy Crane, Brinsley Forde, Denise Pearson, Linda Lusardi, Vicki Michelle, Jan Leeming and John Kettley take part in a special 1980s edition of the general knowledge quiz, in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Plus, a special performance from Jason Donovan. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Up to his eyes in work, an overwhelmed Noel is brushed off by Connie, but is quickly distracted by a troubled teenager in the emergency department, who has inured her hand punching a car door, and her mother Sandi. Meanwhile, Dylan does what he can to help an overwhelmed Faith as she struggles to cope while Luka is in chemotherapy, and things become complicated for Fenisha when a patient on a call-out helps her to make a life changing decision about her unborn baby. Starring Tony Marshall and Olivia D'Lima.(n)

programme.title: Outnumbered
programme.desc: Another guest arrives to stay as the family tries to get rid of Auntie Angela, and Sue has to make a decision about Jake's girlfriend. Elsewhere, Ben and Karen discuss dreams, the Mafia and trampolining bears. Comedy, starring Claire Skinner, Daniel Roche and Ramona Marquez.(n)

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: The bride-to-be goes on a hen party, while Gavin embarks on two stag nights, the second of which reveals shocking secrets about his fiancee that could jeopardise the wedding. Elsewhere, the early return of Stacey's brother Jason horrifies Uncle Bryn. Comedy, starring Mathew Horne and Joanna Page.(n)

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: The day of the wedding arrives and Bryn is still behaving oddly around Stacey's brother. Nessa debates whether to share her big secret and Smithy resolves to give a superb best man's speech - but finds himself overcome with emotion under the spotlight. Mathew Horne and Joanna Page star.(n)

programme.title: The Company You Keep (2012)
programme.desc: A respected lawyer is forced to go on the run when a reporter exposes the truth about him - he is really a member of a radical militant group wanted for questioning in a murder case, who has been living under a fake identity for 30 years. As the fugitive goes in search of his old allies for sanctuary, the reporter delves into his past and uncovers disturbing secrets. Thriller based on the novel by Neil Gordon, directed by and starring Robert Redford, with Shia LaBeouf, Julie Christie and Susan Sarandon.(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Tommy Boyd, Andy Crane, Brinsley Forde, Denise Pearson, Linda Lusardi, Vicki Michelle, Jan Leeming and John Kettley take part in a special 1980s edition of the general knowledge quiz, in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Plus, a special performance from Jason Donovan. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Up to his eyes in work, an overwhelmed Noel is brushed off by Connie, but is quickly distracted by a troubled teenager in the emergency department, who has inured her hand punching a car door, and her mother Sandi. Meanwhile, Dylan does what he can to help an overwhelmed Faith as she struggles to cope while Luka is in chemotherapy, and things become complicated for Fenisha when a patient on a call-out helps her to make a life changing decision about her unborn baby. Starring Tony Marshall and Olivia D'Lima.(n)

programme.title: Outnumbered
programme.desc: Another guest arrives to stay as the family tries to get rid of Auntie Angela, and Sue has to make a decision about Jake's girlfriend. Elsewhere, Ben and Karen discuss dreams, the Mafia and trampolining bears. Comedy, starring Claire Skinner, Daniel Roche and Ramona Marquez.(n)

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: The bride-to-be goes on a hen party, while Gavin embarks on two stag nights, the second of which reveals shocking secrets about his fiancee that could jeopardise the wedding. Elsewhere, the early return of Stacey's brother Jason horrifies Uncle Bryn. Comedy, starring Mathew Horne and Joanna Page.(n)

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: The day of the wedding arrives and Bryn is still behaving oddly around Stacey's brother. Nessa debates whether to share her big secret and Smithy resolves to give a superb best man's speech - but finds himself overcome with emotion under the spotlight. Mathew Horne and Joanna Page star.(n)

programme.title: The Company You Keep (2012)
programme.desc: A respected lawyer is forced to go on the run when a reporter exposes the truth about him - he is really a member of a radical militant group wanted for questioning in a murder case, who has been living under a fake identity for 30 years. As the fugitive goes in search of his old allies for sanctuary, the reporter delves into his past and uncovers disturbing secrets. Thriller based on the novel by Neil Gordon, directed by and starring Robert Redford, with Shia LaBeouf, Julie Christie and Susan Sarandon.(n)

programme.title: Global Questions
programme.sub-title: Global Questions: Coronavirus Crisis: Our Economy
programme.desc: As the Coronavirus takes hold around the world, Global Questions invites its international audience to put their questions to a panel of experts on the impact on the Global Economy. With dire warning of the worst recession since the 1930's what will the fallout be? Are we mortgaging the future of the young for the sake of the old and will we see a return to big government intervention economics? Join Zeinab Badawi and two leading international figures answering your questions from around the world.(n)

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Fun science experiments, including how to work out whether milk is skimmed and information on why sweating keeps skin cool.(n)

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.desc: Following the activities of Laura and Lucy, eight-week-old ring-tailed lemurs who live in Madagascar in a matriarchal society.(n)

programme.title: Vets: Gach Creutair Beo
programme.desc: In Wick, vet Iain Maclean does dental work on a sheepdog, but this patient is very familiar to him - it's his own pet. Plus, a dog with a tumour on his foot and a poorly hen also receive attention.(n)

programme.desc: Ewen MacKinnon looks at the emergence of farming and fishing villages, and reflects on the impact of the Industrial Revolution on rural housing.(n)

programme.title: DIY le Donnie
programme.desc: Donnie MacLeod builds two new gates for Roddy, whose old ones are falling apart. Plus, the DIY enthusiast sorts out a hall radiator badly in need of some care, and begins a project in his own home that will ultimately transform the family's shower room.(n)

programme.title: Eadar Chluich

programme.title: Mach à Seo!
programme.desc: Ramsay MacMahon visits St Andrews where he attended university. Recreating an old student tradition, he plunges into the freezing cold North Sea, feeds penguins at the aquarium and explores the hidden depths of a Cold War bunker.(n)

programme.title: Ameireaga air Film (America in Colour)
programme.desc: Life in the 1940s, when Japan attacked Pearl Harbour and President Roosevelt, freshly elected on his third term, announced that America was now at war. Roosevelt's death, before the end of the conflict saw the presidency to Truman. This was the decade that transforms America into the leader of the free world.(n)

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean explores the deep waters of Rubislaw Quarry, hidden away in the centre of Aberdeen. The quarry opened in 1740 and about six million tons of granite have been extracted from it. With a depth of 140 metres, this has been said to be the largest hole in the ground in Europe.(n)

programme.title: Na Millenni-Gaels
programme.desc: Anton and Alisha are now parents to a little baby girl. They take her to meet the children in the nursery, who are amazed at how small she is and form some interesting ideas.(n)

programme.desc: James is joined by fellow chef Tony Singh for a tour of the Isle of Skye. Tony begins by visiting a local bakery that doubles as an art gallery. programme.title: Tonight
programme.sub-title: Fly Tipping: Britain's Lockdown Problem? Tonight
programme.desc: Fly tipping costs tens of millions to clean up and is a huge problem across the UK. But it could be getting worse in lockdown. With some council tips shut and many authorities operating a skeleton waste collection service, some people are simply dumping their rubbish illegally. Jonathan Maitland investigates.(n)

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show
programme.sub-title: The Martin Lewis Money Show - A Coronavirus Special
programme.desc: Everyday life has changed dramatically with everyone facing more restrictions - and more uncertainty. Martin Lewis presents another live special in which he and Angellica Bell will be examining how coronavirus could affect finances, and taking viewers' questions on specific issues.(n)

programme.title: Ross Kemp: On the NHS Frontline
programme.desc: Ross returns to Milton Keynes Hospital Trust as frontline medical staff brace themselves for the peak of the coronavirus pandemic. With greater numbers of patients testing positive for Covid-19, Kemp witnesses new wards opening to accommodate them and speaks to staff working around the clock to save lives. He also speaks to patients who have come to the hospital with coronavirus symptoms - some will go home and recuperate quickly, but others may have a long road to recovery ahead of them.(n)

programme.title: Beat the Chasers
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts as more studio audience members are called down to play against arguably the most formidable quiz team on TV. First up is a cash builder of five questions, after which they have the option of playing between two, three, four or all of the Chasers for increasing amounts of money - and those brave enough to take on all five could walk away with a huge cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Van Der Valk
programme.desc: Marc Warren steps into the shoes of Barry Foster to take on the role of Dutch detective Van der Valk in this updated adaptation of the Amsterdam-based crime drama. Two seemingly unrelated men are found murdered in different parts of the city on the same day. Are the deaths connected, and if so, could there be a link to the disappearance of a student activist? programme.title: Ainsley's Mediterranean Cookbook
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott continues his tour of the Moroccan city of Marrakesh, beginning with a pottery lesson from a man who has been making pots for more than 50 years. He also hits the souks in search of local pastries, reveals the secret of a perfect cup of mint tea and learns how to prepare a classic tanjia, which is taken to be cooked in the fire of a neighbourhood hammam. Along the way he cooks harissa and preserved lemon chicken skewers, and tagine.(n)

programme.title: How to Keep Your Dog Happy at Home
programme.desc: Advice on looking after dogs during lockdown, when they find themselves spending more time than ever in their owners' company but are limited in their options for going for a walk. In the first episode, a rescue border collie learns some energy-burning tricks from its owner, Ben Fogle and Gabby Logan offer tips on ensuring your pets stay fit. Ben tries out canine yoga with his labrador Storm, while Gaby finds her boxer's talent for jumping may help him get exercise in the garden.(n)

programme.title: The Village

programme.desc: Fly tipping costs tens of millions to clean up and is a huge problem across the UK. But it could be getting worse in lockdown. With some council tips shut and many authorities operating a skeleton waste collection service, some people are simply dumping their rubbish illegally. Jonathan Maitland investigates.(n)

However, this causes a host of problems as the attorney tries to muddle his way through a high-profile divorce settlement. Comedy, with Jim Carrey, Maura Tierney, Justin Cooper, Jennifer Tilly and Cary Elwes.(n)

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news.(n)

programme.title: San Andreas (2015)
programme.desc: In the aftermath of a massive earthquake in California, a Los Angeles rescue-chopper pilot makes a dangerous journey with his ex-wife across the state to try to rescue his daughter. Disaster movie, starring Dwayne Johnson and Carla Gugino.(n)

programme.title: Fast & Furious 8 (2017)
programme.desc: Street racing criminal Dominic Toretto is drawn out of retirement by an encounter with a ruthless cyberterrorist, who recruits him for an international criminal scheme. As he sets out to steal the technology she needs to unleash chaos on the world, he is drawn into conflict with both old enemies and his former gang. Action adventure sequel, starring Vin Diesel, Charlize Theron, Dwayne Johnson and Kurt Russell.(n)

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news.(n)

Action adventure sequel, starring Vin Diesel, Charlize Theron, Dwayne Johnson and Kurt Russell.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des takes Andy on a drinking binge to celebrate his 21st birthday. Jack tries to make Vera see she did not kill Cliff. Jamie is suspended from school. Steve proposes to Vicky.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vicky and Steve try to keep their engagement a secret. Deirdre returns from Morocco after mourning with the Rachids. Raquel organises a party for Andy in the Rovers.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy warns Nick not to say anything about Dave and Kim's affair. Kim meets Nick and he demands money so that he can support Alice in return for his silence.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim panics when Frank calls the police about Nick. Caroline visits Nick in intensive care. Dave finally tells Kim what he thinks of her.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason is called to help Evelyn, a widow who is receiving threats from a corrupt associate of her late husband. Her son-in-law Alan is reluctant to sever his ties with the crook, knowing he provides vital revenue - which forces her into silence. But the police urge Evelyn to cooperate after her car is run off the road and her grandson goes missing. Meanwhile, a Polish visitor arrives in search of a long-lost love.(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.sub-title: A Breath of French Air - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Dispirited by a wet English summer, the Larkins cross the Channel in the hope that the French way of life will have a beneficial effect on the family. David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones star.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby lets a room to a young couple he believes have just got married - unaware that they are not man and wife. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A blizzard forces the sleuthing spinster to spend a night in isolated Sittaford House, situated on a Devon moor. Without explanation, the building's owner, up-and-coming politician Clive Trevelyan, decides to spend the evening in a local hotel - only for a Ouija board to predict his murder. Mystery drama with an all-star cast, including Geraldine McEwan, Robert Hardy, Matthew Kelly, Patricia Hodge and Mel Smith.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dawson helps the officers investigate antiques dealer Mr Brigstocke, who is accused of selling stolen goods. Mason and Wetherby stake out the shop to catch him red-handed, but the situation becomes life-threatening when Brigstocke sets fire to it. Meanwhile, prejudices are stirred by the arrival of some Taoist monks and tragedy strikes local troublemakers Julie and Eddie Tinniswood. With Clare Wille and Joe McFadden.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Dingles argue about their new business venture. Nick regains consciousness in the hospital. Kim manipulates Frank into offering Nick his job back when he is better.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy goes to visit Nick in hospital. Tina cleans the house in preparation for inspection. Dave insists he had nothing to do with Nick's accident. Chris asks Rachel to marry him.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve gives Vicky an engagement ring. Don drives the Duckworths to the funeral. Vicky stuns the guests at the party when she announces that she and Steve are engaged.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz doesn't think Steve and Vicky will be happy together. Alec arrives at the Rovers to sort Vicky out. Bet tells him she is certain Steve is after Vicky's money.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer. Miss Marple is on hand to assist Supt Mallard of the local constabulary, but not before a second victim is claimed. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dawson helps the officers investigate antiques dealer Mr Brigstocke, who is accused of selling stolen goods. Mason and Wetherby stake out the shop to catch him red-handed, but the situation becomes life-threatening when Brigstocke sets fire to it. Meanwhile, prejudices are stirred by the arrival of some Taoist monks and tragedy strikes local troublemakers Julie and Eddie Tinniswood. With Clare Wille and Joe McFadden.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A dentist falls in love with his young assistant. His anxious wife, believing he has made plans to poison her and thereby remove the main obstacle to happiness, calls for Poirot's help. Drama, starring David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A wealthy banker goes missing while walking from his country house to the village post office. Poirot, who has acquired a parrot and developed a taste for conjuring tricks, bets the baffled Inspector Japp £5 he can solve the mystery without leaving his own apartment. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: An idyllic summer day in Oxford is torn asunder when a parcel bomb claims the life of eminent mathematician Adam Capstone, a genius in the field of 'Knot Theory'. As Lewis and Hathaway investigate, they discover that, while the victim's academic reputation was beyond reproach, his private life was far more dubious. It emerges Capstone was a man of carnal appetites, who had a string of affairs with young students, betraying his chemistry professor wife Elizabeth, providing her with a clear motive to do away with her cheating spouse. As the detectives work to identify the killer bomber, the case becomes a personal challenge for Lewis, who fears he may not be rewarded with another contract should he fail to impress Moody before his six-month trip to New Zealand. Detective drama, starring Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox, with Zoe Tapper, Tristam Summers and Oliver Lansley.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.sub-title: Piece of My Heart - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the body of investigative journalist Matt Barber is found in a holiday chalet in a remote village, Banks and his team are initially confronted by an overwhelming number of leads. As the detective identifies a connection between the victim and a mysterious death in the 1980s connected to the surviving members of a cult rock band, he must begin to unpick decades of lies. To make matters worse, the deceased man's father is former senior police officer Jack Barber, who is now a candidate in the local commissioner elections - and Morton is convinced he is obstructing their inquiry. Stephen Tompkinson stars.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.sub-title: Piece of My Heart - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. With lawyer David Hornby now a suspect, Cabbot is forced to admit the father of her baby doesn't know about her pregnancy. She then discovers band member Martin Soames' confession in the John Gaunt case file is missing and that Matt Barber's father Jack was the original investigating officer, leading Helen to believe she has the evidence she needs to push the former policeman harder. But when Banks learns of a mysterious tape recording that appears to link Jack with the Crystal Kiss, he realises he's in a race to find it if he is to have any hope of uncovering the truth. Stephen Tompkinson stars.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An American tourist's body is discovered in her hotel room, apparently dead from a heart attack - but a valuable Anglo-Saxon jewel she intended to donate to the Ashmolean Museum is missing and several interested parties are acting strangely. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Simon Callow, Roberta Taylor and Kenneth Cranham.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: New tenant Osborne seemingly dies after eating a bowl of Miss Jones' home-made soup, plunging the household into mourning - but Rigsby is the one who ends up being measured for a coffin. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter.(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)
