programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Part two of two. The guys sort through their haul from Italy, packing a shipping container with retro scooters, antique furniture and religious artefacts.(n)

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Sean Kelly and the bidders are looking for treasure in Somerset's storage units, where a bouncy castle and an air boat are among the finds.(n)

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Heavy D is in town and is prepared to risk having his fingers burned for the chance of a big score, while auctioneer Sean Kelly keeps a watchful eye.(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank visit a contact living in Sicily, who offers to show them around a treasure-filled house.(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The duo travel to London, where they consider buying a rare motorbike and a telephone box with a risque history.(n)

programme.title: Red Bull Soapbox Race
programme.desc: The steep streets of Cincinnati's Mount Adams neighbourhood play host to the crazy cart designs, 40,000 people going wild and plenty of crashes.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters find three small cars at an affordable price - a task that involves pestering bankers, harnessing large dogs and almost getting arrested. James May meets American rally driver Ken Block, and Jeremy Clarkson tests out the Mercedes SL Black. Comedian Michael McIntyre is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Mega Air
programme.desc: The world's biggest air package handling facility at UPS Worldport, which sorts two million items per night for worldwide delivery. Fireman also tackle a mock air freighter inferno.(n)

programme.title: Cop Car Workshop
programme.desc: The work of a team of mechanics who build bespoke cars for the Merseyside Police. Emergency surgery is needed when a brand-new prisoner van gets on the wrong side of a rooftop protestor. Boss Mick spends some time behind bars - all in the name of research, while the mounted unit needs help to get to the Liverpool-Everton derby.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond and James May test the Porsche Panamera against the Royal Mail's delivery service, racing a first-class letter to see which travels faster over the full length of the British Isles. Jeremy Clarkson gets chased by the British Army in some of their most deadly toys, and multi Olympic gold medal-winning sprinter Usain Bolt is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May each buy a rear-wheel-drive coupe for £1,500 and find themselves entered into a French ice race. Richard discovers a way to fill the waiting time for flights by inventing the sport of airport-vehicle racing, Jeremy gets behind the wheel of the BMW Z4, and actress Sienna Miller is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies challenges Hugh Dennis to wield a blade, while Mel Giedroyc destroys a mannequin. Alex Horne helps coax the contestants.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts the comedy panel show in which two teams of celebs try to call each other's bluff with a few stories about themselves that might be true or might be out-and-out porkies. Joining team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are comedians Jo Brand, Roisin Conaty and Paul Foot, and TV wildlife presenter Ray Mears.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.sub-title: Would I Lie to You? At Christmas
programme.desc: Rob Brydon introduces a seasonal special of the comedy panel game. Screen hardman Ray Winstone, comic Josh Widdicombe, maths whizz Rachel Riley and actor Ricky Tomlinson join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack to hoodwink their opponents with a few festive fibs or facts about themselves. Does one of the contestants really apologise to their boiled egg before smashing its head in? Who apparently writes Christmas cards with their feet? And did one of the line-up really prevent their bandmate joining the Beatles?(n)

programme.title: Insert Name Here
programme.desc: Actor Stephen Mangan, TV presenter Mel Giedroyc, comedian Nish Kumar and historian Suzannah Lipscomb join host Sue Perkins and captains Richard Osman and Josh Widdicombe on the comedy panel show. In this edition, the two sides answer questions about famous people past and present who share the name Mary, with the winners earning the right to pick the greatest bearer of the moniker - prompting comic banter, funny facts and unusual stats as the teams vie to outdo each other.(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Guests Colin Lane, Sarah Millican and Noel Fielding join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'numbers' in this extended episode. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting.(n)

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: The Dwarfers face disaster when they are surrounded by a Simulant Death Ship and a fleet of Annihilators while hiding in an asteroid, and it is up to Arnold J Rimmer to save them. Comedy series, starring Craig Charles, Chris Barrie and Robert Llewellyn.(n)

programme.desc: Coverage from Barcelona of an event where teams create vehicles without engines and race downhill.(n)

programme.desc: Before lights out to start the Formula 1 season in Australia, the programme gains access behind the scenes at F1 team Haas as they pack tonnes of cars and gear for air freighting.(n)

programme.title: Cop Car Workshop
programme.desc: Mick Sweetmore's team tries to fix a radio issue in a bid to keep the new pursuit cars on the tail of Merseyside's criminals. There's a surprise visit from the fleet's oldest member, and the bosses get dirty hands when things go wrong on the road.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are each challenged to find a classic car at auction for £3,000. Exploring whether they are more fun to drive than modern alternatives, the trio then take the vehicles to Majorca to compete in a rally. Jeremy also test-drives the BMW Z4 and Nissan 370Z, and AC/DC frontman Brian Johnson is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May are challenged to create a TV advert for Volkswagen, but soon fall out as James concentrates on detailed information on fuel economy and Jeremy wants something more exciting. Richard Hammond explores an alternative to Germanic sports saloons and tests the Vauxhall VXR8 Bathurst, and former US chat show host Jay Leno is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts the comedy panel show in which two teams of celebs try to call each other's bluff with a few stories about themselves that might be true or might be out-and-out porkies. Joining team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are EastEnders star June Brown, comedians Aisling Bea and Seann Walsh, and football presenter Adrian Chiles.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.sub-title: Would I Lie to You? The Unseen Bits
programme.desc: A compilation of extra material from the comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon. Team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by guests including Claudia Winkleman, Miles Jupp, Bob Mortimer, Jo Brand, Adil Ray, Kirsty Wark, Amanda Abbington and Carol Vorderman to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves.(n)

programme.title: Insert Name Here
programme.desc: Wildlife expert Chris Packham, actor Paul Feig, comedian Jon Richardson and historian Kate Williams join host Sue Perkins and captains Richard Osman and Josh Widdicombe on the comedy panel show. In this edition, the two sides answer questions about famous people past and present who share the name Ben, with the winners earning the right to pick the greatest bearer of the moniker - prompting comic banter, funny facts and unusual stats as the teams vie to outdo each other.(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Guests Lucy Porter, Frankie Boyle and Ross Noble join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'next' in this extended episode. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are each challenged to find a classic car at auction for £3,000. Exploring whether they are more fun to drive than modern alternatives, the trio then take the vehicles to Majorca to compete in a rally. Jeremy also test-drives the BMW Z4 and Nissan 370Z, and AC/DC frontman Brian Johnson is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's French Odyssey
programme.desc: The connoisseur of local food takes a gastronomic tour along the canal systems of southern France, from Bordeaux to Marseille. He samples seafood and regional cuisine, as well as exploring a variety of traditional recipes, including artichoke salad with poached egg, eels in cider, and bass with beurre blanc.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's French Odyssey
programme.desc: The connoisseur of local food explores the banks of the River Garonne on board a 100-year-old converted barge, and savours the delights of French provincial cooking including magret de canard and petit sale. Other dishes featured are clams in white garlic sauce, eels in parsley and garlic butter, and steak tartare.(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: In Louisiana, Danielle calls the shots from HQ when the guys find themselves in over their heads, and Frank strikes a side deal that leaves a permanent mark.(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank finally get access to their white whale pick, while in Texas, the guys meet a collector who has filled an entire town with his mammoth stockpile.(n)

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: US host Sean Kelly oversees bidders in Lewes battling to win potential treasures found in storage containers, lock-ups and garages.(n)

programme.title: Eddie Eats America
programme.desc: In Houston, Eddie faces the 'grim reaper' in a crawfish challenge featuring the fearsome reaper pepper. He also devours some southern comfort food and pulls a plane down a runway.(n)

programme.title: Eddie Eats America
programme.desc: Eddie Hall follows in Rocky's footsteps with physical challenges at a famed Philadelphia boxing gym - and his very own hunger games in the infamous Philly Cheesesteak Challenge.(n)

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: People forced out of their homes in Honduras by the notorious MS-13 gang plead their case for asylum in America. Officers discover 3,000lb of marijuana hidden in a cargo truck.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are in Russia as the motoring magazine returns, taking part in a race across the urban landscape of St Petersburg. James defends the honour of the car in a very small Renault, Richard tries to prove that pedalling is faster by riding a bicycle, while Jeremy attempts to demonstrate that water is the quickest way across town by piloting a hovercraft. To make matters more complicated, all three presenters are racing not only one another but also the Stig, who is making his way toward the finish line by public transport. Plus, the new Lamborghini Huracan is put through its paces and singer-songwriter Ed Sheeran takes to the test track in the Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters embark on a road trip across Australia's Northern Territory in fast, luxurious GT cars, with Jeremy Clarkson choosing a BMW M6 Gran Coupe, Richard Hammond picking a Bentley Continental GT V8 and James May opting for a Nissan GT-R. The trio set out on an adventure that takes in crocodile-infested rivers, a race around a vast opencast mine and an encounter with one of the world's longest lorries, concluding with an unusual challenge involving a farm, their cars and 4,000 cows. Plus, 24 star Kiefer Sutherland takes to the track in the Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner presents the comedy panel show, which sees chef and MasterChef judge John Torode, comedian Russell Howard and actress Katherine Parkinson compete to have their pet hates banished for ever. Their gripes include massive pepper grinders, grumpy kids and DJs who join in at the end of a song.(n)

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner invites comedy actress Catherine Tate, X Factor contender-turned-television host Rylan Clark-Neal and veteran actor Nigel Havers to vent their spleen about people and objects that upset them. Their gripes include hipster restaurants, set-up paparazzi shoots and Labour Party leader Jeremy Corbyn.(n)

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner invites comedian David Mitchell, tennis coach Judy Murray and Countryfile presenter Anita Rani to vent their spleen about people and objects that upset them. Their gripes include sniffing, walking and eating, and sugar lumps.(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy accidentally runs over a pet rabbit belonging to the daughter of an important client, so she needs someone to help cover her tracks. Unfortunately, the best people she has are Lee and Daisy. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Katy Wix.(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee, Lucy and Daisy go on a skiing trip to Eastern Europe, but as usual disaster isn't far behind. The trio of friends find themselves stuck in a cable car with a handful of other nervous tourists - one of whom just happens to be pregnant. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Katy Wix.(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy becomes a relationship counsellor, so Lee and his father take advantage of her new-found skills in an effort to patch things up between them. Can they finally forgive and forget? Bobby Ball guest stars as Frank, with Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Katy Wix.(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Loyiso Gola, Milton Jones, Miles Jupp, Ellie Taylor and Josh Widdicombe join host Dara O Briain and regular Hugh Dennis for the topical comedy show, looking back at the week's events through the medium of fast-thinking and improvisation.(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Rob Beckett, Ed Gamble, Milton Jones, Romesh Ranganathan and Tiff Stevenson join host Dara O Briain and regular Hugh Dennis for the topical comedy show, looking back at the week's events through the medium of fast-thinking and improvisation. First aired in September 2016.(n)

programme.title: Dave Gorman: Terms and Conditions Apply
programme.sub-title: Shot Through the Face with a Canon
programme.desc: Using his trademark, powerpoint-comedy and mischievous games, Dave invites Katherine Ryan, Suzi Ruffell and Rufus Hound to help him make sense of our insensible world.(n)

programme.title: Operation Gold Rush with Dan Snow
programme.desc: Dan Snow, Felicity Aston and Dr Kevin Fong build a wooden boat, following original designs for the vessels used during the Gold Rush. This vessel must carry them across hundreds of miles of treacherous lakes and rivers, including the infamous One Mile Rapids, a narrow canyon of white water and jagged rocks.(n)

programme.title: Operation Gold Rush with Dan Snow
programme.desc: Dan Snow, Felicity Aston and Dr Kevin Fong finally arrive at the end of their gruelling journey in Dawson City. They set up a traditional mining camp in the heart of the gold fields, before facing their next challenge. They have just five days to find gold in an area that has already been heavily mined, and can only use the back-breaking methods of the 19th-century early pioneers.(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston travels to Sardinia to meet the scientists and archaeologists who have evidence that suggests giants once existed. Researchers have discovered abnormally large human skeletons in networks of elaborate caves, mines and tombs in Sardinia, China, the United States, northern India and South America. However, if the race did exist, it remains unclear as to why it has seemingly been eradicated from history.(n)

programme.title: Inside Hitler's Killing Machine
programme.desc: The establishment of the concentration camps, revealing how quickly a system of concentration populations turned into a tool for mass murder.(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The longest retreat in British military history, when Allied troops defending Burma in 1942 were confronted by Japanese forces well prepared for jungle warfare. Narrated by Laurence Olivier.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An advanced German battleship that sank in a Norwegian fjord during the Second World War, the Miles Glacier Bridge in Alaska, and a mysterious concrete giant in Bulgaria.(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough investigates the opportunities tides create for marine life, showing giant stingrays gliding on currents for a rest, South African sea snails surfing the waves in pursuit of a meal, crabs playing football on a beach, and bottlenose dolphins embarking on a dangerous game of catch. In the oceanic world each turn of the tide can mean life or death for its inhabitants.(n)

programme.title: Trains That Changed the World
programme.desc: The railroads ended slavery in the USA, but trains can be agents of oppression too. The British Empire built railways to rule the world, enabling it to control the empire.(n)

programme.title: Wildest Africa
programme.desc: Exploring the Ngorongoro crater in northern Tanzania, considered to be one of Africa's most treasured wonders, including a look at the wildlife that inhabits the area.(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Exploring the wealth of marine life that inhabits the ocean's edge, dealing with the dangers of an ever-shifting environment. David Attenborough observes nesting seabirds in east Russia, Patagonian killer whales, Newfoundland caplin fish, walruses, and Brazilian green turtles - which make their annual expedition to an island in the middle of the Atlantic to lay their eggs.(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The extension of the Panama Canal to accommodate the world's largest ships, which drew inspiration from the work of 17th-century engineer Hugues Cosnier.(n)

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: The history of the concentration camp, charting its origins as a place to hold political prisoners through its development into a mechanised factory for mass murder. The first episode features the accounts of Polish inmates held there prior to the Holocaust, Jewish survivors and a former SS officer, who discusses being placed in charge of a firing squad.(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Luftwaffe's bombing raids on Britain's provincial cities, when Coventry and Plymouth were hardest hit, although Portsmouth, Glasgow, Sheffield and Bristol also suffered heavy casualties. Narrated by Laurence Olivier.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Abandoned projects of the space age, including the HARP gun in Barbados and the mountainside Barcroft Observatory in California.(n)

programme.title: The Story of Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: A look back at how supporting characters including Boycie and Trigger developed over the years. Featuring contributions from cast members and famous fans.(n)

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Devious royal butler Edmund Blackadder steps in to foil a plan by new prime minister Pitt the Younger to bankrupt his master the Prince Regent - but as usual his intentions are not particularly honourable. Rowan Atkinson, Hugh Laurie and Tony Robinson star.(n)

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Dr Johnson seeks Prince George's support in publishing his new dictionary, but brainless Baldrick burns the only copy. Edmund sets out to save the English language by rewriting the great man's masterpiece - but he has only one night to complete it. Comedy, guest starring Robbie Coltrane, with Rowan Atkinson, Tony Robinson and Hugh Laurie.(n)

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: After Edmund scorns the exploits of the Scarlet Pimpernel, two noblemen bet him 1,000 guineas that he can't go to France and rescue an aristocrat. Instead, he finds the Comte de Frou Frou at Mrs Miggins' pie shop and takes him to the French embassy, but ends up being arrested by a revolutionary who has assassinated the ambassador. Comedy, guest starring Nigel Planer, Tim McInnerny and Chris Barrie.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A highway built in the Brazilian jungle as an escape route in the event of an emergency at a nuclear power station, a Soviet fortress and Ellis Island hospital.(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Neil Oliver meets historian Collum MacNeil on the deserted island of Mingulay, and discovers why it was abandoned in 1912 after 2,000 years of habitation. Miranda Krestovnikoff learns the truth behind the classic Ealing comedy Whisky Galore! on Eriskay, and chemist Mike Bullivant tries to recreate a 19th-century experiment to turn peaty soil into oil.(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston travels to Jerusalem in search of Solomon's Temple. When the Romans sacked the city in 70AD, the fabled Treasure of Solomons was believed to have been looted and taken back to Rome. However, it is possible it was hidden away by the High Priests. Archaeologists Dr Shimon Gibson and Kate Raphael have dedicated their lives working out what actually happened, and discuss whether it is possible that Solomonic and Roman treasures lie undiscovered under Jerusalem.(n)

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: The history of the concentration camp, charting its origins as a place to hold political prisoners through its development into a mechanised factory for mass murder. The first episode features the accounts of Polish inmates held there prior to the Holocaust, Jewish survivors and a former SS officer, who discusses being placed in charge of a firing squad.(n)

programme.title: Trains That Changed the World
programme.desc: How the steam train reached the height of its importance in Europe just as fresh innovations were being made in America, with groundbreaking trains like the Burlington Zephyr.(n)

programme.title: Remarkable Rwanda
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the diverse and different aspects of the African country, from magnificent mountain gorillas to one of the world's largest colobus monkey colonies.(n)

programme.title: South Pacific
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the region's islands, which are home to a vast array of extraordinary wildlife. The programme reveals how remote human communities native to the area are surrounded by harsh terrain and subjected to extreme weather conditions. Benedict Cumberbatch narrates.(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: How the world's biggest ship, designed to install oil platforms and lay pipelines, was inspired by a logging machine, a wire rope and 1940s shipbuilding techniques.(n)

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: A look at the early stages of genocide at the camp, examining the role of commander Rudolf Hoess in the process. Despite later claiming he was only following orders, Hoess was instrumental in the development of Auschwitz for mass murder, arranging for Soviet prisoners to be brought in as construction workers and scouring Europe for more victims to kill. Featuring contributions from survivors.(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How the morale of the Third Reich, boosted by Germany's initial military successes, was shattered by the devastating defeat at Stalingrad, relentless Allied bombing raids and an attempt on Hitler's life. Narrated by Laurence Olivier.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The world's most impressive disused railway bridges, including the biggest wooden trestle bridge in the world, which was closed down due to hurricane damage.(n)

programme.title: WW2 - Battles for Europe
programme.desc: Examining the work of the American forces who launched a huge offensive to blast a gap in the German lines and break through to liberate France.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Third Reich
programme.sub-title: Secrets of the Third Reich: The Nazi Gold Train
programme.desc: Part one of two. The search for a missing train containing Nazi treasure, which was believed to have been hidden in the mountains of Poland.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A marooned ironclad structure off the coast of England that hides a dramatic story of war and resilience, and a facility in the heart of America that helped tear apart a nation.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Series exploring ruined engineering projects that have been put to alternative use, including the site of the Chernobyl disaster and a Nazi holiday complex.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Neil Oliver admires ginger seals at Walton-on-the-Naze, while Nick Crane examines the area's fossil record. Miranda Krestovnikoff explores research into the thornback ray on the Thames Estuary and how it aids conservation. The team also visits Felixstowe, the UK's busiest container port, where HM Revenue and Customs uncover a stash of cocaine smuggled among furniture.(n)

programme.title: Deadliest Crash: Disaster at Le Mans
programme.desc: Documentary examining what caused Pierre Levegh's Mercedes to crash into the crowd during the 1955 Le Mans 24-hour race, killing more than 80 people - an accident that remains the worst in the history of motor racing. Among the theories discussed are the use of untested technologies by the car's manufacturers, the addition of a lethal fuel additive and allegations of reckless driving by future Formula One world champion Mike Hawthorn, who won the race with Jaguar team partner Ivor Bueb. Narrated by Jack Deam.(n)

programme.title: Nazi Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: The theories surrounding the death of Hermann Goering, who managed to cheat the hangman by taking a cyanide capsule on the day before his scheduled death.(n)

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Heavy haulage experts Ian Bagladi and Jamie Turnbull need to move a classical but abandoned 63-year-old, 70-tonne locomotive, through the English countryside.(n)

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A widow reluctantly parts with her husband's treasured collection of Rolls Royces and Bentleys, and an impulse buyer finds one of the cars a new home.(n)

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of a car auction business. A near-perfect 50s Chevy panel van and a very desirable low-mileage 80s Brooklands Capri tempt auctioneer Derek out of Yorkshire.(n)

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: The team struggles to explain how a DAF 33 gearbox works, and office manager Sarah attempts to bring order to the auto memorabilia sale.(n)

programme.title: Yellowstone
programme.desc: Following the wildlife that inhabits the famous American national park, a lost world of deep forests, vast plains and bubbling geysers. The first episode focuses on a variety of species as they struggle to survive the rigours of a freezing six-month winter, revealing that although the herds of elk and bison are weakened by the cold, one animal gets stronger - the wolf.(n)

programme.title: Yellowstone
programme.desc: Summer returns, transforming the landscape of the famous national park from a frozen wilderness into the ideal environment for newborn animals. As temperatures rise, grizzly bears teach their young the secrets of survival - but even at this time of year, snow can sweep down from the mountains without warning.(n)

programme.title: Yellowstone
programme.desc: An early dusting of snow signals the end of autumn, a time when the forests are a blaze of red and gold and the animals prepare for the coming cold. The elk face hungry wolves as they move down from the mountains to feed in the valleys, while beavers rush to defend their habitat against the destructive ice, repairing dams and stocking underwater larders before the waters freeze over.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Folk performances by artists including Pete Seeger, Tim Buckley, Joan Baez, Bob Dylan and Leonard Cohen.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Music from the Top of the Pops archives, featuring performances by acts including the Foundations, Procol Harum, the Bee Gees, the Rolling Stones, and Dave Dee, Dozy, Beaky, Mick and Tich.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Music from the flower power era, featuring bands including Herman's Hermits, the Crazy World of Arthur Brown, the Alan Price Set, Manfred Mann and the Rolling Stones.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Archive clips featuring solo artists, including music by performers as diverse as Sandie Shaw, Dusty Springfield, Long John Baldry, Lulu, Tom Jones, Brenton Wood, Cliff Richard, Cilla Black and Peter Sarstedt.(n)

programme.title: Princess Margaret: The Rebel Royal
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary profile of the Queen's sister, whose life and loves reflected the social and sexual revolution that transformed Britain during the 20th century. With archive footage and interviews, the series follows Margaret's life as she redefined the image of the modern princess, and begins with her birth in 1930. The abdication of her uncle propelled Margaret to second in line to the throne, but her life would be shaped by dramatic social change as Britain entered a more egalitarian era after the Second World War.(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Castle
programme.desc: Documentary chronicling a year in the existence of 1,000-year-old Windsor Castle and its inhabitants, from the housekeeper and chefs to the Queen herself, who has allowed her public and private life to be documented more extensively than ever before. The state visit of former President of France Jacques Chirac sees the staff organise a banquet and a performance of a West End musical inside the building - but protesters outside threaten to mar proceedings.(n)

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built
programme.desc: Tim Dunn explores the disused tube station Down Street in Mayfair, that played a crucial tactical role during the Second World War, and tours Rotterdam's new Centraal station with the man who designed it.(n)

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: The design and manufacture of the Transit, Ford's ubiquitous white van, and the full-length, all-weather riding coats produced by Driza-Bone.(n)

programme.title: Salvage Hunters: Classic Cars

programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, with cameras capturing their instant reactions and the lively discussions that are prompted by the week's biggest and best shows.(n)

programme.title: Joe Lycett's Got Your Back
programme.desc: The comedian fights the good fight on behalf of the British public, tackling viewers' consumer problems, no matter how big or small. In this edition, Made in Chelsea's Jamie Laing helps Joe launch the UK's first ever consumer rights rave, while the show's fearless assistant Mark Silcox tests the slogan of a high street bank. Joe also infiltrates hackable smart tech and launches a musical raid on the offices of the world's largest online retailer. Plus, comedian Lucy Beaumont spies on CCTV itself.(n)

programme.title: Paul Hollywood Eats Japan
programme.desc: The Great British Bake Off judge takes a culinary tour of Japan, discovering the importance of food in Japanese culture. He starts in Tokyo, where he gets a lesson in dining etiquette, visits themed restaurants, and discovers the growing fashion for dining out and even performing karaoke alone. He meets an Australian wrestler and heavy metal singer named Lady Beard, who joins him on a madcap karting ride around the city's streets.(n)

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: Members of the Dias family take on the Lunsfords, as a conservative, stay-at-home wife whose military-minded husband runs their household swaps lives with an entrepreneur mum from a same-sex relationship.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie: Keep Crafting and Carry On
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp masters the art of quilling to fashion the letter R for her son's name sign, and reveals how all the letters come together to form the sign made with love. She also goes online to chat with her old friend Zack to find out the best way to craft a wire creature, and calls on the crafting community to send in images of their lockdown makes.(n)

programme.title: Reasons to Be Cheerful with Matt Lucas
programme.desc: Harry Hill-penned comedy entertainment series, celebrating everything that is keeping the Great British public, and some celebrity chums, entertained at home. Matt showcases the ability of men, women, children, celebrities and possibly even some pets to amuse themselves during this self-enforced downtime, as he's joined by a plethora of key workers, families at home and famous faces including Tyson Fury, Kevin McCloud, Rachel Riley, Heston Blumenthal, Tom Allen, Jon Snow and Basil Brush.(n)

programme.title: Beat the Chef
programme.desc: Trained chef Frederick Forster goes up against a home cook, with the pair having to create the most flavourful Asian miso broth and crispiest tempura. Andi Oliver presents.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fun-loving Sonya Smith is the first to host the dinner-party challenge from Reading, Berkshire, and aims to win over the guests with a Touch of Sparkle theme. Aware her cooking skills leave a lot to be desired, she hopes to bungle her way through the night, and though she is amused by the kitchen chaos, not everyone is impressed.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Londoner Glen Wilkie hosts the second dinner party of the week in Reading, Berkshire, and is confident of victory. However, a disaster with his dessert gets the day off to a bad start, and his main course, which he has chosen to call Bambi on a Plate, leaves one guest in tears - while another flatly refuses to eat it.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mature student Richard Howse hosts the third dinner party in Reading, using a menu and set of instructions provided by his girlfriend to guide him. A treasure hunt brings out Glen Wilkie's feelings about Serine Daou, Sonya Smith dislikes some of the gluten-free food and, for the third night in a row, Sabi Phagura is served a plate of meat she will not eat.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The fourth dinner party in Reading sees fitness fanatic Sabi Phagura putting her healthy regimen to one side to host a bhangra-themed evening. Unfortunately, neither the traditional Indian food on the table nor the entertainment seems to impress the guests.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Corset-maker Serine Daou hosts the final night of the culinary competition in Reading, Berkshire, and hopes to impress with her burlesque-themed party. When the gourmet burgers she serves fail to impress, she must rely on the evening's entertainment to put her back on course for the £1,000 prize.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge searches for a stash of wine, said to be hidden by the Germans during the Second World War. Fiona starts renovating a dining room for a visit by the mayor. Philip and Angelina continue to prepare their chateau and its grounds for a big event, but they have a lot to do and are running out of time.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: It's crunch time for Jonathan and Michael who are facing their investors, but their newest idea - to keep goats - does not go down well. Philip and Angelina host the big anniversary party they've been spending weeks preparing for, but with broken toilets and 60 guests to contend with, how will it go? Plus, single mother Fiona battles to get her dining room ready to show off to the mayor.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge follows the fortunes of Brit Mandy and her American business partner Steve, who have decided to build a swimming pool to entice more paying guests to their stunning cliff-edge chateau in the Dordogne. Desperate to increase income at his chateau, Michael invites Angel Adoree to help out with an experiment to do afternoon tea for the locals.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: As Jonathan and Michael prepare their chateau for the big wedding, they have a sewage problem that threatens to take the whole first floor out of action.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Jonathan and Michael prepare for a wedding day with 45 guests. With the first guests of the season about to turn up, the pool continues to be a problematic nightmare for Mandy and Steve. Dick's wife Angel shows some clever, cheap, wedding tips - but will Dick and the kids like them?(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Rosaria bought Bella Luna to realise her son Gianfranco's ambition to become a chef, but the Italian restaurant's future looks bleak due to his lack of experience in the kitchen combined with her refusal to acknowledge the eatery's many flaws. Gordon Ramsay heads to Easton, Pennsylvania, to help the troubled pair and save the business from financial disaster.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three restaurants he helped that were among the biggest challenges he faced, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or turned back into nightmares. First he meets sisters Rita and Lisa to see if they have continued serving fresh instead of frozen food at Boston's La Galleria 33, before heading to the Olde Hitching Post in Hanson, Massachusetts, to discover whether controlling owner Tom has kept his promise and handed over the reins to his daughter Andrea. Finally, Gordon goes back to the Prohibition gastro pub in Everett, Washington, to catch up with owner Rishi and learn if she has ditched belly-dancing in favour of running her business.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef heads to Babylon in New York, where the peace of Long Island is regularly shattered by the volatile Pellegrino family, proprietors of Peter's Italian Restaurant. Gordon finds owner Tina's work to save the eatery from oblivion is frequently ruined by the behaviour of her brother, a tyrannical front-of-house manager whose violent temper constantly undermines staff morale.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef tries to reverse the fortunes of the Mixing Bowl diner in Bellmore, New York, where a talented cook's work is being undone by the manager's marketing plans - putting the finances in dire straits.(n)

programme.title: My Wife and Kids
programme.desc: Kady's soccer team complains that Janet is pushing them too hard, so Michael steps in and replaces her as coach - but his easy-going approach fails to fill them with the will to win.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The documentary about landlord and tenancy issues catches up on some of the stories featured so far. Emmanuel reveals what happened after he returned from a year working abroad to find himself £24,000 in mortgage arrears and unable to get into his own property. Andy made a mistake when he rented out his north London home to someone he knew, only for his so-called friend to sublet the property, pocket the profits and pay Andy nothing. What has happened since?(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Letting agent Paul has a tenant in Lancashire who is behind with his rent and won't return any calls, but the situation is much worse than he could possibly have imagined when he heads into the upstairs bedroom. Harrow enforcement officers Andrew and Ozge are called to a local salon, where a leak from the upstairs flat is disrupting Masi's ability to run her shop. Once the landlord agrees to give them access, they discover terrible conditions and a back yard piled with rubbish.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The debt collectors take action to serve a writ issued for the organiser of a music festival.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Max and Steve in East London try to recover over £7,000 owed by a restaurant owner for an unpaid gas bill, while officers Gareth and Mitch in Gloucestershire seek just under £3,000 from the owner of a car wash. Stewart and Vic are in Manchester trying to recover nearly £5,000 owed by a blinds and shutters supplier to a dissatisfied customer.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Documentary series giving an insight into the world of debt collection from the perspectives of debtors, creditors and those who collect the payments. Brian and Graham have been warned to expect trouble as they evict some tenants from a south London flat, one of whom has a reputation for violence. Everything goes smoothly until the enforcement agents make a shocking discovery in a child's bedroom. Paul and Steve head to Slough to collect wages owed to a freelance worker, but the debtor is less than happy to find the high-court enforcement officers on his doorstep and things get physical.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Sheriff Paul Bohill tackles the case of a small debt owed by ex-footballer Neil 'Razor' Ruddock to a kennel owner, before being forced to evict a school dinner lady in south London. Meanwhile, in the north of England enforcement agent Stewart MacCracken heads out with trainee Iain Taylor to confront a Yorkshire builder they need to get payment from at his home, while a reptile shop owner hit by online competitors urgently needs to pay a debt for the feed company he relies on to keep his animals alive.(n)

programme.title: House
programme.desc: Dominika and House research each other's families as they try to convince US immigration their marriage is genuine. Meanwhile, the team competes to become second in command, and a relationship counsellor collapses during a speaking engagement, before displaying some strange behaviour. Medical drama, starring Hugh Laurie.(n)

programme.title: House
programme.desc: The doctor receives a shock when his mother arrives unexpectedly at the hospital with her latest partner. Meanwhile, the team battles to save a blind man who has been struck down by a mysterious illness while preparing to propose to his girlfriend. Guest starring Billy Connolly.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Stewart and Elmore are met with lies and aggression when they track down nearly £3,000 owed by a garage mechanic, while Steve and Ben encounter a retired couple who are being forced to leave their home. Stuart and Vic revisit a young debtor who owe £750 for a laptop computer, and in Oxfordshire, Paul and Steve chase £30,000 owed by a world-famous Formula One racing team.(n)

programme.title: World's
programme.desc: Unbelievable motoring moments caught on camera, featuring a man who demolished 23 buildings in his customised bulldozer and a hit-and-run driver who smashed through a plate-glass cafe window, pinning a customer against the counter in the process. Plus, caravan banger racing, scary parking and joyriding in a tank. Vicki Butler-Henderson provides expert analysis on the wheel-spinning clips, while eyewitnesses and those who survived tell their stories.(n)

programme.title: Hurricane Hell: Superstorm
programme.desc: In just one month, Houston, Florida, Puerto Rico and the Caribbean were changed forever. In summer 2017, three monster hurricanes swept in from the Atlantic one after another, shattering storm records and killing hundreds of people. Experts address the issue of whether these superstorms will become more prevalent.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Following a tip-off, Harrow Enforcement officers are shocked to discover an industrial-scale cannabis farm in a house. programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the fatal shooting of two professors, which occurred soon after one of the academics campaigned against the treatment of women in the Middle East. Their inquiries lead them to a Muslim with strong beliefs - and an extreme dislike of females. Crime drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The team investigates the stabbing of a teacher whose body has been found in a caretaker's cupboard. The hunt for her killer leads to the discovery that she was caught up in a love triangle at school involving a student with multiple identities. Legal drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Ducky identifies two dismembered bodies as those of a prosecutor and a sheriff he worked with on a rape case 10 years previously, and realises he could be the killer's next target. Despite Gibbs' attempts to protect him, he is later kidnapped - and the team's search for clues leads to a macabre showdown at a funeral home. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A woman claims to have witnessed the murder of a sailor, but a lack of physical evidence causes the team to question what actually took place. Spurred by his gut instinct, McGee perseveres in his search for clues and soon discovers there is more to the case than he could have imagined.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The detectives investigate the murder of a woman whose scantily clad body is found on the Upper East Side. Initially believing her to be a prostitute, they learn she was a doctor who had been handing out unauthorised doses of a powerful drug. The hunt for her killer leads the team into a web of deceit and blackmail, with a Russian mobster and his son seemingly pulling the strings.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A bookmaker is arrested on suspicion of murdering his business partner - and his attorney puts together an unconventional case that effectively turns the whole trial into a political statement. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Sam Waterston.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A notorious drug dealer is murdered and suspicion falls on two possible culprits - a priest with a reputation as a vigilante, and the father of a teenager who died of an overdose. Briscoe and Green attempt to discover whether either of them took the law into his own hands. Starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The body of a petty officer is found lying in bed next to a barman - but he insists she is not the woman he brought home with him the previous night. The investigation leads Gibbs and the team to the victim's sister (R'n'B star Mya), who is a singer in a rival bartender's dance club. Drama, starring Mark Harmon.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team try to find out who is responsible for sending a parcel containing a pair of blue human eyeballs to the home of a petty officer who works in Intelligence. However, the recipient of the organs commits suicide during the course of the inquiry, which leads the investigators to suspect one of his lecturers, who is working for the CIA in Panama.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Rollins and Carisi investigate when a man is castrated and left for dead. Langan has devastating news for Benson.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Detectives Benson and Stabler check the handbag of a suspected rape victim for identification and discover the severed finger of a small child in a plastic bag. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A woman asks Benson and Stabler to help her find out who made her daughter pregnant. The girl, who has Down's syndrome, is unable to give much information, especially while being pressured by her distraught mother to abort the baby.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Foster parents are accused of murdering a five-year-old in their care, but it seems their son's video game obsession could be the real key to her death - until the victim's biological mother snatches her other daughters from the house. Hard-hitting police drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: When a woman is found dead following what appears to have been an auto-erotic encounter, Benson and Stabler make a link with the case of a male stripper claiming to have been raped during a hen night. While Cabot prosecutes the sexual assault case, the detectives find that the girl's seemingly accidental death was actually murder. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A car chase results in the discovery of a census taker's body in the boot of a vehicle, but the driver claims not to know how he got there. programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A homeless man arrested for stabbing an heiress admits breaking into the victim's house, but claims he was not involved in the crime. Meanwhile, Abby puts her technical skills to the test by trying to repair a camera that was broken during the victim's fall.(n)

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: Law & Order

Award-winning police drama, starring Sam Waterston.(n)

programme.desc: A witness to a jewellery store robbery that went tragically wrong fails to appear in court. Police drama, guest starring Mandy Patinkin of Criminal Minds fame, with Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach.(n)

programme.desc: New York's district attorneys investigate the mysterious death of a stockbroker who was dating the daughter of a ruthless cosmetics mogul - and find themselves caught up in a wrangle over whether HRT withdrawal can be used as a defence in law. Police drama, guest starring Lucie Arnaz, with Jerry Orbach and Sam Waterston.(n)

programme.desc: A petty officer is killed while competing in a swimsuit contest, raising fears she may have fallen victim to a jealous rival, or even a stalker. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette.(n)

programme.title: Ransom

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Stabler is personally affected by the team's investigation into the case of a woman whose unborn child was removed from her body via a crude Caesarean section two months before its due date. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

Crime drama, starring David Caruso, Eva La Rue and Jonathan Togo.(n)

programme.desc: A famous, but ailing, playwright tempts fate by proposing to Jessica - and almost immediately finds himself charged with murdering his business manager. Starring Angela Lansbury.(n)

programme.desc: A priest and government agents using a safe house get Jessica involved in a complicated case, in which she hears the confession of a dying assassin who is connected to a recent murder. Angela Lansbury stars.(n)

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness some of the best and worst behaviour on the nation's motorways. The series focuses on Auckland, the country's largest urban area, and features motoring chaos including major traffic collisions, escaped animals and pedestrians on busy roads. programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Raisin
programme.sub-title: Agatha Raisin and the Quiche of Death
programme.desc: Ashley Jensen plays the title role in this quirky crime drama, based on the novels of MC Beaton. Bored with her new life in the Cotswolds, former London-based PR whizz Agatha Raisin re-doubles efforts to ingratiate herself with the local community. However, when entering its annual quiche-making competition, she inadvertently finds herself a suspect in a murder case. With her reputation tarnished and hopes of romance with a dashing ex-army neighbour seemingly thwarted, Agatha feels she has little choice but to clear her name by unmasking the real culprit. With Hermione Norris, Robert Bathurst, Katy Wix and Mathew Horne.(n)

programme.title: Take That Live: Wonderland
programme.desc: A concert filmed at the O2 Arena in London, which was part of the band's 2017 tour in support of their eighth studio album Wonderland. Howard Donald, Mark Owen and Gary Barlow played several nights at the venue, perfoming much loved hits such as Relight My Fire, Patience, Pray, The Flood, and How Deep Is Your Love?. The latter was recently re-recorded with Barry Gibb for their 2018 album, Odyssey.(n)

programme.desc: Kate gets zapped by a scientist's shrinking ray, and ends up so small that Kim and Kylie cannot find her.(n)

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: The girls take part in a fashion show, but soon discover that someone intends to sabotage the event.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Halvar is kidnapped by his arch rival Sven, and Vic has to find a way to make the ship move without a sail.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Animated series about a young viking. A gang of pirates leads Vic's father into a trap, but the youngster hatches a plan to trick them.(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The lively zebra becomes a superhero, but his friends begin to take advantage of him.(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The waterhole becomes too crowded to play Coconut Catch, so Zigby decides to build a new swimming pool.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Gilby challenges Vic to a climbing contest, with the winner joining the crew of the Drakkar.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Faxe steals sausages from an Icelandic king, which results in the whole crew getting held to ransom.(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: Elle the elephant hurts her trunk and Zigby decides to set up a hospital on the island.(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The lively zebra and his friends build a boardwalk so they can get to the water park easily.(n)

programme.title: Property Ladder
programme.desc: A property developer's plans for a hi-tech bachelor pad in Battersea grind to a halt after he starts building without his neighbours' permission. Sarah Beeny follows Jonathan Moon's progress after he is forced to pay thousands in compensation and sees his costs spiral out of control, forcing him to give up his own home and car.(n)

programme.title: Friend or Foe (1982)
programme.desc: Two Second World War evacuees face a dilemma when they meet a German pilot who has been shot down. Family drama, starring John Bardon and Stacey Tendeter.(n)

programme.title: Elenya (1992)
programme.desc: Aa 12-year-old half-Italian Welsh schoolgirl stumbles across a wounded German airman during the Second World War and secretly cares for him - only for tragedy to strike as the local townsfolk discover what she has been up to. Drama, starring Pascale Delafourge Jones, Klaus Behrendt, Margaret John and Sue Jones Davies.(n)

programme.title: Very Important Person (1961)
programme.desc: A pompous Allied scientist ends up in a PoW camp after crash-landing during a reconnaissance mission over Germany. His uncompromising attitude and a series of misunderstandings initially earn him the enmity of his fellow inmates, but his popularity improves when he devises an audacious escape plan. Second World War comedy, with James Robertson Justice, Stanley Baxter, Leslie Phillips and Eric Sykes.(n)

programme.title: Friend or Foe (1982)
programme.desc: Two Second World War evacuees face a dilemma when they meet a German pilot who has been shot down. Family drama, starring John Bardon and Stacey Tendeter.(n)

programme.title: The Ronnie Wood Show
programme.desc: The guitarist talks to his current Faces bandmate Mick Hucknall. The duo perform a version of Bob Dylan's One of Us Must Know, and Ronnie shares his memories of Jimi Hendrix.(n)

programme.title: Faking It
programme.desc: Individuals take up the challenge to reinvent themselves completely.(n)

programme.title: Property Ladder
programme.desc: Sarah Beeny meets first-time developers Alex and Vonny Shelley, who try to transform a run-down mid-terrace house in Nottingham on a budget of just £5,000. After six weeks, costs spiral out of control and their friends are too busy to help with the building work.(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Dr Saleyha Ahsan reveals new research that could cure snoring, while Michael Mosley joins volunteers in a study to discover if changing your diet can lower cholesterol as much as drugs like statins - with surprising results. Surgeon Gabriel Weston sees the results of an unusual treatment for a hospital superbug and Dr Chris van Tulleken investigates skin care products and asks if they do more harm than good.(n)

programme.title: Obsessed with My Body
programme.desc: With British boys now apparently spending 30 per cent more time in the bathroom than teenage girls and taking twice as many selfies, this documentary follows some of those in search of the perfect body. From bodybuilding and eyebrow waxing to make-up, the film also examines how far they will they go to achieve perfection. And what happens when things are taken to extremes?(n)

programme.title: Fox
programme.sub-title: Oh Dear - Oh Dear - Oh Dear!
programme.desc: Drama about a London gangster and his family. Starring Peter Vaughan, Ray Winstone and Bernard Hill. Guest starring Jim Carter (Downton Abbey). Originally broadcast in 1980.(n)

programme.title: Sex Ed (2014)
programme.desc: A teacher arrives at a new school, and is appalled to discover his teenage students are receiving no sex education. He takes it upon himself to provide the facts of life, despite objection from the local preacher. However, his plan is complicated by his still being a virgin and falling in love with a pupil's older sister. Comedy, starring Haley Joel Osment and Lorenza Izzo.(n)

programme.title: Long Weekend (1978)
programme.desc: A couple take a camping holiday at a remote beach to try and rebuild their struggling relationship. Their disrespect for the wildlife and the environment provokes the wrath of a supernatural presence, and the torment they endure from it brings tensions between them to the surface. Horror, starring John Hargreaves and Briony Behets.(n)
